page: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is a dedicated CPU server instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC processor family. It features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1 data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This hardware configuration is paired with 62.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 360 GB local SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks position the ccx43 in the average to strong tiers. Single-core CPU performance is strong, with stress-ng single-core benchmarks scoring 3056.68, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested instances. Multi-core stress-ng performance is average at 24552.03. Memory bandwidth is consistent, with read-only small block cached speeds at 1167.25 GB/sec and large block uncached read and write speeds at 52.44 GB/sec and 37.18 GB/sec respectively. The system demonstrates strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 13.85 GB/sec, while multi-threaded decompression and gzip compression perform in the average range.

For specialized workloads, the instance handles database operations and web serving reliably, achieving 5107.44 in Passmark database operations and 16.99 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Redis SET operations reach 21.67 million operations per second. In machine learning tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 269.05 tokens per second with a small 135M LLM, though performance drops to a poor 4.17 tokens per second for prompt processing on a large 70B model. This profile makes the ccx43 highly cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and medium-scale database hosting that require dedicated CPU resources without the premium of memory-optimized configurations.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is a dedicated CPU server instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores from the AMD EPYC x86_64 processor family, running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 62.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 3.91 GB per core, and includes 360 GB of local SSD storage. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, scoring 3056.68 in stress-ng benchmarks, and average multi-core capabilities. Memory bandwidth is stable, with uncached read speeds of 52.44 GB/sec. While it lacks a GPU and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, its dedicated resource allocation ensures predictable execution. The ccx43 is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is a dedicated AMD EPYC server with 16 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and 360 GB SSD storage. It delivers strong single-core CPU performance and balanced resources for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx43 is a dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring 16 vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and 360 GB SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server offering balanced compute and memory resources with local SSD storage for predictable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
62.0 GB of system memory and 360 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance and bzip3 decompression capabilities
Average multi-core performance and stable memory bandwidth
Best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and database hosting
